title: Reformer
biography: Dorothea Dix was an American reformer who led a crusade to create state psychiatric hospitals and humane treatment of the mentally ill. Her advocacy led to the creation of dozens of mental health facilities across the United States and Europe.
aiSummary: Dorothea Dix is a psychiatry specialist at New Jersey State Lunatic Asylum. Based in Trenton.

timeline.title: Published Memorial to the Legislature of Massachusetts
timeline.description: Exposed the horrific conditions of mentally ill patients in jails and poorhouses

timeline.title: Federal mental health legislation
timeline.description: Persuaded Congress to consider federal land grants for mental health facilities
